body:
## Context

Follow-up spun out of #423 / PR #424. During that work the reviewer asked that *all* algorithms in the codebase be "truly general" — built from the smallest possible meaningful substitutions (rules) via actual reasoning, rather than memoization (fixed lookup tables / per-case keyword whitelists).

The #423 installation-conversion algorithm is architecturally general (single install-step IR + format-agnostic renderers, no per-repository lookup table — the 100 regression repos all flow through the same pipeline). But while implementing it, several memoization-flavored heuristics were noticed across the solver that deserve their own generalization pass. Filing here so #424 stays scoped to the README↔script conversion task.

## What "memoization vs reasoning" means here

A memoized recognizer answers by matching against a hardcoded enumeration (extend the list → support one more case). A reasoning recognizer derives the answer compositionally from structure, so unseen-but-well-formed inputs are handled without editing a table.

## Concrete spots observed

1. **Command recognition is a fixed prefix whitelist.** `src/solver_handlers/installation_conversion.rs::looks_like_command` accepts a line only if it starts with one of an enumerated `PREFIXES` set (`apt `, `brew `, `cargo `, `npm `, …). Any tool not in the list is silently dropped from the IR. A structural recognizer (token shape, code-span provenance in Markdown, pipe/`&&` composition) would reason about whether a line *is a command* rather than memorizing tool names.
2. **Step descriptions are a fixed mapping.** `describe_command` maps substrings (`git clone` → "Clone the repository", etc.) to prose. General intent inference from the command verb/object would scale beyond the enumerated cases.
3. **Intent dispatch is a fixed ordered table.** `src/solver_dispatch.rs::SPECIALIZED_HANDLERS` is a hand-ordered list of `(intent, handler)` pairs; each new task class appends one entry. This is the central place where "one solution per memorized intent" lives, versus synthesizing a handler from smaller reusable rule primitives (the meta-algorithm direction from #423).

## Proposed direction

- Replace the command whitelist with a structural command recognizer (provenance- + shape-based) and measure it against the existing #423 regression corpus (must stay green) plus adversarial prose lines.
- Generalize `describe_command` to derive descriptions from the parsed verb/object instead of a substring table.
- Audit the remaining specialized handlers for memoized keyword lists that could be expressed as composable rules, and prototype expressing one existing handler purely through the #423 meta-algorithm rule primitives as a proof of concept.

## Acceptance criteria

- A written audit listing each handler that relies on a fixed enumeration vs. compositional rules.
- At least the installation-conversion command recognizer generalized away from the prefix whitelist with no regression in the 100-repo corpus and added false-positive (prose) coverage.
- A documented plan (or prototype) showing one existing coding handler reconstructed from the meta-algorithm rule primitives.
title: Generalize solver recognizers from memoized lookup tables to compositional rules (follow-up to #423)
